ogTitle: Levenstrath vs Lewis Ponds: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Levenstrath and Lewis Ponds.
heading: Levenstrath vs Lewis Ponds — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Levenstrath, New South Wales and Lewis Ponds, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lewis Ponds has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Levenstrath (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Levenstrath or Lewis Ponds safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Levenstrath has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Lewis Ponds scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Levenstrath scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Lewis Ponds scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Levenstrath or Lewis Ponds?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lewis Ponds has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.43% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
